content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 3, 2015

Samantha Olson
Attorney III
Office of Chief Counsel
P O Box 100
Sacramento, CA 95812-0100

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-15-197

Dear Ms. Olson:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the North Coast Regional Water Control Board (the "Water Board") and Board Member Valerie Minton regarding conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your questions are general in nature and not limited to specific governmental decisions, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.²

Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090.

QUESTION

Is salary from the Sonoma Resource Conservation District (the "SRCD") potentially disqualifying income for purposes of the Act's conflict of interest provisions?

CONCLUSION

Salary from the SRCD is not income under the Act because the SRCD is a local governmental agency. Accordingly, in itself, salary from the SRCD does not disqualify Board Member Minton from taking part in decisions by the Water Board implicating the SRCD.

_______________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

File No. 1-15-197
Page No. 2

FACTS

You represent the Water Board and have been authorized to seek advice on behalf of Board
Member Valerie Minton. In addition to being a member of the Water Board, Board Member Minton
is also program director at the SRCD. As the program director, Board Member Minton receives a
salary from the SRCD. Because a variety of future decisions by the Water Board may overlap with
RCD activities related to agriculture and water quality, Board Member Minton is seeking assistance
as to whether the SRCD is a governmental agency and whether salary received from the SRCD is
potentially disqualifying for purposes of the Act's conflict of interest provisions.

Regional Conservation Districts are organized for the purposes of "formulating and putting
into effect a statewide program of soil and water conservation and related natural resource
conservation." (Public Resources Code Section 9001.) Public Resources Code Section 9003 also
states, "resource conservation districts are legal subdivisions of the state and, as such, are not-for-
profit entities." The Sonoma RCD has adopted a conflicts of interest code, and its employees,
including Board Member Minton, file Statements of Economic Interest as required by the code.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any state or local public official from making, participating in
making, or using his or her official position to influence a government decision in which the official
has a financial interest. A public official has a "financial interest" in a government decision, within
the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect on one or more of the public official's interests. (Section 87103.) Interests from which a
disqualifying conflict of interest may arise include only the following:

• An interest in a business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment of
$2,000 or more (Section 87103(a)); or in which the official is a director, officer, partner,
trustee, employee, or holds any position of management (Section 87103(d)).

• An interest in real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or
more. (Section 87103(b).)

• An interest in a source of income, including promised income, which aggregates to $500 or
more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(c); Regulation 18703.3.)

• An interest in a source of gifts to him or her if the gifts aggregate to $460 or more within 12
months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(e).)

• An interest in the official's personal finances, including those of his or her immediate
family. This is known as the "personal financial effects" rule. (Section 87103)

In this case, you have asked only if the salary from the SRCD constitutes potentially
disqualifying income under Section 87103(c). As expressly defined by the Act, income does not
include "salary and reimbursement for expenses and per diem received from state, local or federal

File No. I-15-197
Page No. 3

government agency . . . ." (Section 82030(b)(2).) Therefore, the pertinent question is whether the
SRCD is a local governmental entity.

Under the Act, a "local governmental agency" is any "county, city or district of any kind
including school district, or any other local or regional political subdivision, or any department,
division, bureau, office, board, commission or other agency of the foregoing." (Section 82041.)
Moreover, Section 87300 requires every local and state agency to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code.
You have stated that the SRCD has adopted a conflict of interest code designating SRCD officers
and employees who must file conflict of interest statements as required by Section 87300. Thus, the
SRCD has determined that it is a local agency subject to the Act. Pursuant to Section 87301,
"Conflict of Interest Codes shall be formulated at the most decentralized level possible . . . ." Where
an entity has determined it is a governmental agency, we generally defer to the agency's
determination unless there is reason to suggest that the determination is incorrect. Moreover, as you
have noted, the Commission has historically regarded officers and employees of regional
conservation districts as governmental employees. (See Staroveytov Advice Letter, No. A-14-149;
Guarino Advice Letter, No. A-05-068.)

Accordingly, for purposes of the Act's conflict of interest provisions, the SRCD is a local
governmental agency and Board Member Minton's does not have a potentially disqualifying
interest in the SRCD as a source of income.³

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: Brian G. Lau
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

BGL:jgl

³ We caution that an effect on an official's governmental salary may still be disqualifying under limited
circumstances as a material and foreseeable financial effect on the official's personal finances. Generally, a decision by
the Water Board will not have a reasonable foreseeable material effect on Board Member Minton's personal finances
merely because the decision overlaps with SRCD activities. However, potential effects on Board Member Minton's
personal finances can only be analyzed on a decision-by-decision basis. If additional facts suggest that a decision by the
Water Board will complicate Board Member Minton's compensation from the SRCD, Board Member Minton may want
to seek additional assistance identifying the specific decision.

Is salary from the Sonoma Resource Conservation District (the "SRCD ") potentially 
disqualifying income for purposes of the Act's conflict of interest provisions?

governmental agency. Accordingly, in itself, salary from the SRCD does not disqualify Board 
Member Minton from taking part in decisions by the Water Board implicating the SRCD.2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or fonnal 
written advice. (Section 83114: Regulation l 8329(c)(3).)
